decisions: Retained all 3 daily logs (04-21, 04-22, 04-23): oldest is 3 days old, well within 7-day retention window. First deletion eligibility is 2026-04-28 for the 04-21 log.

No content promoted to any tier: reviewed daily/2026-04-21.md (1 bootstrap entry), daily/2026-04-22.md (2 consolidation entries), daily/2026-04-23.md (3 consolidation entries) — all contain only maintenance activity with no user interactions, decisions, or learnings to extract.
observations: Brain has been active for 4 days (since 2026-04-21) with zero user interactions — all memory tiers (core, semantic, episodic, procedural) remain at their bootstrap-empty state
Daily log chain is healthy and continuous from 04-21 through 04-24, but all entries are consolidation-only activity
The consolidation pipeline is functioning correctly but has not yet been exercised with real content to promote
recommendations: No action needed until first user interaction populates memory tiers with real content
Monitor for first user session — that will be the true test of the promotion pipeline from daily logs to core/semantic/episodic/procedural tiers
processImprovements: [self-critique] The consolidation process is running reliably but has been exercised only with empty/maintenance content for 4 consecutive days. There is no way to validate that the promotion logic (daily → core/semantic/episodic/procedural) works correctly until real user sessions occur. The process itself may be over-reporting 'pass' on criteria that are trivially true due to no content.

[proposal] Consider adding a 'content-richness' metric to the self-assessment that explicitly flags when consolidation runs process zero user-generated content, to distinguish healthy-but-idle from healthy-and-working.
